protein_name: Essential multidomain scaffolding protein required for normal development. Recruits channels, receptors and signaling molecules to discrete plasma membrane domains in polarized cells. May play a role in adherens junction assembly, signal transduction, cell proliferation, synaptogenesis and lymphocyte activation. Regulates the excitability of cardiac myocytes by modulating the functional expression of Kv4 channels. Functional regulator of Kv1.5 channel. Belongs to the MAGUK family. Abundantly expressed in atrial myocardium (at protein level). Expressed in lung fibroblasts, cervical epithelial and B-cells (at protein level). Widely expressed, with isoforms displaying different expression profiles. 4 alternatively spliced human isoforms have been reported. Note: This description may include information from UniProtKB.
